title: Gambeya lungi
taxonomy: Gambeya lungi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. The family Sapotaceae is a large group of flowering plants that include over 900 species of trees and shrubs. Gambeya lungi is native to tropical Africa, specifically in countries such as Cameroon and the Republic of the Congo. It is a member of the genus Gambeya, which is a small group of plants that are primarily found in central and western Africa. The classification of Gambeya lungi is further supported by its unique characteristics, such as its distinctive leaves and flowers.
uses: Gambeya lungi has been reported to have medicinal uses, particularly in traditional African medicine. The plant is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In some parts of Africa, Gambeya lungi is used to prepare a paste that is applied topically to treat skin irritations and wounds. The plant's leaves and bark are also used in herbal remedies to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal properties and potential health benefits.

intro: Gambeya lungi, also known as African bush mango, is a tropical tree native to West Africa, characterized by its small, yellowish-green fruit and edible pulp.
